specific_mechanism: Ferromagnets respond to applied field via discrete domain-wall jumps; the cumulative jumps produce a measurable Barkhausen noise signature that reveals microstructural pinning sites
llm_application: LLM weight-update jumps under fine-tuning produce a measurable 'Barkhausen' signature that reveals capability-pinning sites — i.e., parameter regions resistant to update because of training-data history.
novelty_claim: No published 2024-2026 LLM work formalizes tribology basin-stability mapping as proposed.
